description: Validates GNOME Files (Nautilus) is functional on a fresh Bluefin boot.
All shared steps come from qecore common_steps; Nautilus-specific checks live in
gnome_files_steps.py.

elements.name: Files launches and window is accessible
Home folder is in the sidebar

steps.name: Home folder is in the sidebar

elements.name: Navigating to home folder shows contents

steps.name: Navigating to home folder shows file listing

elements.name: Back button returns to previous location after navigating to Downloads
New folder dialog opens via keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+N
Search bar opens via Ctrl+F

steps.name: File search bar is open in Files

elements.name: Files closes cleanly via Ctrl+W
No Nautilus coredump after session start

name: GNOME Files smoke tests

description: Validates desktop notifications can be delivered without GNOME Shell regressions.

elements.name: Toast notification banner can be dismissed

Dismiss the notification via gdbus CloseNotification
Notification banner is no longer showing via Shell.Eval

steps.name: A test desktop notification is sent via gdbus
No gnome-shell notification journal errors are present

name: GNOME notification smoke tests

Power settings panel is accessible
Accessibility settings panel is accessible
Settings closes cleanly via Ctrl+Q

elements.name: No critical journal errors at boot

elements.name: bootc status shows a known image reference

steps.name: bootc status shows a valid image reference

elements.name: Writable system storage has more than 15 percent free space

steps.name: Writable system storage has at least "15" percent free space

ujust is available and lists at least one task

description: Verifies Bluefin's MIME handler assignments so file types resolve to the
expected desktop applications via the shared MIME database.
Runner: qecore-headless (local to VM).

elements.name: HTML files open in Firefox
PDF files open in Papers (GNOME Papers)

steps.name: xdg-mime query default for "application/pdf" returns a document viewer

elements.name: PNG images open in Loupe or GNOME image viewer

steps.name: xdg-mime query default for "image/png" returns an image viewer

elements.name: Plain text opens in GNOME Text Editor

steps.name: xdg-mime query default for "text/plain" returns a text editor

elements.name: Video files open in a video player

steps.name: xdg-mime query default for "video/mp4" returns a video player

name: xdg-open MIME type handler registration

description: Validates XWayland starts on demand when a native X11 client connects and
runs without crashing.  Uses glxgears from glx-utils because it is a pure
X11 GLX client (links libX11, calls XOpenDisplay) that ships on stock
Fedora/GNOME images.

elements.name: XWayland starts on demand for an X11 client

result.error_message: ASSERT FAILED: XWayland is already running: 6084 /usr/bin/Xwayland :0 -rootless -noreset -accessx -core -auth /run/user/1001/.mutter-Xwaylandauth.F7HQS3 -listenfd 4 -listenfd 5 -displayfd 6 -initfd 7 -byteswappedclients -enable-ei-portal

elements.name: X11 client can use the XWayland display

steps.name: xprop can query the X root window

result.error_message: ASSERT FAILED: xprop -root failed (rc=0, exit=1): Warning: Permanently added '127.0.0.1' (ED25519) to the list of known hosts.

elements.name: XWayland incurs no coredump after the X11 client closes

name: XWayland smoke tests
